ref, toRef, and toRefs in Vue 3
ref, toRef, and toRefs are everyday tools for Vue 3 Composition API developers, but the three are subtly different. This post walks through how each one works.
The JavaScript a = b = c Interview Question, Finally Explained

Multiple left-hand assignment — also called chain variable assignments — is one of those classic JavaScript interview questions. Stuff like a = b = c that looks innocent but trips people up. So I wrote a post digging into how it actually works, including the nasty version that mixes in hoisting and call-by-sharing object references.
Mac "Port xxx is Already in Use" — How to Free It Up

Building a project locally and hitting "Port 5000 is already in use"? This post covers how to identify what's holding a port on Mac (lsof), how to kill it (kill PID), and the special case of macOS reserving port 5000 for AirPlay Receiver — including the exact System Settings toggle to free it.
axios is a really handy AJAX package, and Nuxt.js helpfully integrates it for you — but the way you use it is a bit different.
Local Dev Hits "Failed to read localStorage: Access is denied" — Here' s the Fix

Hit a weird Chrome console error during local dev — "Uncaught DOMException: Failed to read the 'localStorage' property from 'Window': Access is denied for this document." Writing it down. Turns out Chrome's third-party cookie blocking is treating http://localhost:5500 as blocked. The fix is allowing the domain in Chrome's site settings — and as a bonus, the same setup works fine via 127.0.0.1:5500.

Anyone who's used the internet has seen them — HTTP status codes. If you're heading toward backend dev, you really should know your way around them. This post covers the five categories and walks through the most common ones you'll actually encounter — 200, 301, 302, 400, 401, 403, 404, 500, 503 — with Express.js examples for each.
Receiving Google AdSense Wire-Transfer Payouts (Richart / Taiwan Example)

Five-minute walkthrough of setting up Google AdSense wire-transfer payouts. The bank used here is Richart (Taishin International Bank, Taiwan) — the specifics are Taiwan-flavored, but the general flow (find your foreign-currency account details, fill in AdSense's wire details form, wait for the $100 threshold, claim the incoming wire) applies to AdSense payouts in any country.

When you copy-paste a URL somewhere, you usually get a description and title preview. In plain SPA that doesn't really work — you're stuck with the default head. How does Nuxt.js handle this? Let's talk about head configuration.
SocialVIP — A Linktree-Style Multi-Link Page Built for Taiwan (LINE Pay Tipping)

A friend suggested adding a tip jar link to my blog footer instead of Liker. Looking around I found SocialVIP — a Linktree-style multi-link page that's localized for Taiwan and supports LINE Pay tipping (which makes it more usable than Linktree for Taiwan-based creators). Short post on the basics — also relevant if you're in a market where LINE Pay matters more than Stripe.
A Friendly Intro to Functional Programming in JavaScript

Functional Programming is honestly one of the more fun paradigms to play with. But why do we even need it? In short, it's about avoiding a bunch of problems caused by side effects. So I figured I'd write a really beginner- friendly, really simple intro to Functional Programming in JavaScript.
